understandingCredits.title: Entendendo Créditos
understandingCredits.description: Os créditos servem como moeda universal do Kortix para operações da plataforma. Cada ação que teus agentes de IA executam—de analisar dados a gerar código—consome créditos com base na complexidade da tarefa e nos recursos necessários.

howCreditsWork.title: Como os Créditos Funcionam
howCreditsWork.description: Os créditos são consumidos com base nos recursos que teus agentes de IA usam:

aiModelUsage.title: Uso do Modelo de IA
aiModelUsage.description: O principal fator de consumo de créditos
aiModelUsage.content: Diferentes modelos de IA têm custos diferentes com base em suas capacidades e uso de tokens. Os créditos são consumidos para tokens de entrada (teus prompts e contexto), tokens de saída (respostas do agente) e variam por nível do modelo (Claude, GPT, etc.).

pricingModel.title: Preços
pricingModel.description: As taxas variam por tipo de serviço
pricingModel.content: Aplicamos uma margem sobre os custos dos fornecedores para cobrir infraestrutura da plataforma, segurança e desenvolvimento contínuo. Modelos de IA têm margem de 20%, uso de ferramentas (pesquisa web, scraping, etc.) tem margem de 50%, pesquisa de imagens tem margem de 100%, e se usares a tua própria chave API, aplica-se uma taxa fixa de 10%.

gettingMoreCredits.title: Obtendo Mais Créditos
gettingMoreCredits.description: Existem várias formas de obter créditos no Kortix:

monthlySubscription.title: Créditos de Assinatura Mensal
monthlySubscription.description: Incluídos no teu plano pago e renovados automaticamente a cada mês. Estes são créditos com expiração.

topUpCredits.title: Créditos de Recarga
topUpCredits.description: Compra créditos adicionais quando precisares. Estes não expiram e estão disponíveis para membros premium.

promotionalGrants.title: Concessões Promocionais e de Eventos
promotionalGrants.description: Créditos bônus de eventos especiais, promoções ou indicações. Estes não expiram.

refunds.title: Reembolsos
refunds.description: Créditos devolvidos devido a problemas técnicos ou tarefas falhadas. Estes não expiram.

typesOfCredits.title: Tipos de Créditos
typesOfCredits.description: O Kortix usa dois tipos de créditos para te dar flexibilidade em como geres teu uso:

expiringCredits.title: Créditos com Expiração
expiringCredits.content: Estes créditos estão incluídos na tua assinatura paga e são renovados automaticamente a cada mês na data da tua assinatura. Eles expiram no final de cada ciclo de cobrança e são sempre consumidos primeiro antes de quaisquer créditos sem expiração.

nonExpiringCredits.title: Créditos sem Expiração
nonExpiringCredits.content: Estes créditos nunca expiram e são transferidos de mês para mês. Eles incluem compras de recarga, reembolsos e concessões promocionais. Os créditos sem expiração são usados apenas após os teus créditos com expiração serem esgotados.

creditPriority.title: Prioridade de Créditos:
creditPriority.description: Quando usas o Kortix, os créditos com expiração são consumidos primeiro. Apenas após os teus créditos com expiração se esgotarem, os créditos sem expiração serão usados.

appHomeEnterprisePage.heroTitle: Enterprise-grade control over an AI workforce.
appHomeEnterprisePage.heroDescription: Most AI platforms ask you to trust a black box. Kortix is the opposite: every agent, action, and permission is scoped, logged, reviewable, and yours to deploy wherever your data has to live.
appHomeEnterprisePage.whyDescription: The enterprise page should lead with what makes Kortix different: governance for agents, policy gates around every action, and a control plane your team can review.
appHomeEnterprisePage.moatTitle: Enterprise AI is not just a user-management problem. It is an action-management problem.
appHomeEnterprisePage.differentiatorAgentGovernanceTitle: Governance that covers agents, not just people.
appHomeEnterprisePage.differentiatorAgentGovernanceDescription: RBAC is table stakes when humans are clicking buttons. Kortix scopes every permission to humans and agents alike: tools, secrets, connectors, files, and projects.
appHomeEnterprisePage.differentiatorExecutorTitle: Every action passes through a policy gate.
appHomeEnterprisePage.differentiatorExecutorDescription: The Executor brokers each tool call an agent makes. Set the rule per action: run automatically, require a human approval, or block outright.
appHomeEnterprisePage.differentiatorGitTitle: Nothing is a black box, because it is all code.
appHomeEnterprisePage.differentiatorGitDescription: Agents, skills, triggers, and policies live as reviewable files in a repo. Every change is a commit, every rollout is traceable, and every mistake can be rolled back.
appHomeEnterprisePage.securityTitle: The controls your team already expects.
appHomeEnterprisePage.securityDescription: Keep the checklist tight and scannable. These are proof points for security review, not the whole story.
appHomeEnterprisePage.identityAccessTitle: SSO, provisioning, roles, and policy grants.
appHomeEnterprisePage.runtimeAuditTitle: Logs, isolated execution, and protected secrets.
appHomeEnterprisePage.checklistSamlDescription: Okta, Azure AD, or any SAML provider with JIT provisioning.
appHomeEnterprisePage.checklistScimDescription: Automated user and group provisioning with per-account bearer tokens.
appHomeEnterprisePage.checklistRbacDescription: Account and project roles, SCIM-sourced groups, and policy-based grants.
appHomeEnterprisePage.checklistAuditDescription: Full audit log with CSV/JSONL export and signed SIEM webhooks.
appHomeEnterprisePage.checklistSandboxesDescription: Every session runs in an ephemeral VM with server-side credential resolution.
appHomeEnterprisePage.checklistSecretsDescription: Secrets are encrypted at rest, injected at runtime, and kept out of logs.
appHomeEnterprisePage.deploymentTitle: Deploy where your data has to live.
appHomeEnterprisePage.deploymentDescription: Bring your own models and provider keys in every deployment, including locally hosted and open-weight models.
appHomeEnterprisePage.deploymentManagedCloudDescription: Hosted and maintained by Kortix. Fastest path to production.
appHomeEnterprisePage.deploymentPrivateVpcDescription: Single-tenant inside your own cloud perimeter.
appHomeEnterprisePage.deploymentOnPremDescription: Fully isolated on your infrastructure for sensitive environments.
appHomeEnterprisePage.onboardingTitle: What working with us looks like.
appHomeEnterprisePage.onboardingDescription: Make procurement feel concrete: a short path from scoping to security review to first agent rollout.
appHomeEnterprisePage.onboardingScopingDescription: We map your tools, teams, risk boundaries, and first production use case.
appHomeEnterprisePage.onboardingSecurityDescription: Your team gets the documentation, deployment details, and answers they need.
appHomeEnterprisePage.onboardingDeploymentDescription: Cloud, VPC, or on-prem stood up with the Kortix team alongside you.
appHomeEnterprisePage.onboardingRolloutDescription: First agent live quickly, then expansion department by department.
appHomeEnterprisePage.closingTitle: Tell us your constraints. We will show you how Kortix runs inside them.
appHomeEnterprisePage.closingDescription: Custom pricing, a dedicated engineer, security review support, and an SLA for teams running agents at scale.
appHomeEnterprisePage.closingFootnote: Open source, SSO, RBAC, on-prem, no lock-in.
appHomeEnterprisePage.onboardingProcessTitle: A clear path from security review to first agent in production.

appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line69JsxTextNotToolsNotAgentsNotWorkflo: Não ferramentas. Não agentes. Não workflows. Empresas — com departamentos de engenharia, times de operações, funções financeiras, suporte ao cliente, motores de crescimento — que operam sozinhas. Agentes fazendo o trabalho real, 24/7, sem um humano tocar em cada tarefa.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line93JsxTextThisIsNotAForecastItIsOur: Isto não é uma previsão. É nossa meta operacional — a proporção em que operamos hoje e a proporção que buscamos construir com cada empresa com que trabalhamos. Humanos não são removidos. São elevados. Eles definem a direção, revisam entregas e tomam as decisões finais sobre o que importa. Os agentes cuidam do resto.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line105JsxTextWhatWeMeanByFactory: O que queremos dizer com fábrica
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line111JsxTextAFactoryIsASystemForTurnin: Uma fábrica é um sistema que transforma entradas em saídas em escala, de forma confiável e repetida, sem depender da presença de uma pessoa específica. É isso que construímos para empresas.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line118JsxTextYouFeedTheFactoryYourGoals: Você alimenta a fábrica com seus objetivos, suas ferramentas, seu contexto, seus padrões. A fábrica produz: código entregue, tickets fechados, contratos redigidos, livros reconciliados, campanhas outbound, pesquisas sintetizadas, candidatos recrutados. Todos os dias. Esteja você no escritório ou dormindo.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line138JsxAttrBodyEveryCompanyHasProcess: Toda empresa tem processos que dependem da atenção humana. Nós os encontramos — filas de suporte, ciclos de relatórios, sequências outbound, verificações de QA, code review, processamento de faturas — e os tornamos legíveis. Você não pode automatizar o que ainda não nomeou.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line143JsxAttrBodyEachProcessBecomesAnAg: Cada processo se torna um agente. Não um chatbot. Não um wrapper de prompt. Um especialista — com identidade, permissões, ferramentas, memória e regras de disparo próprias. Um agente de engenharia que entrega PRs. Um agente financeiro que fecha os livros. Um agente de suporte que resolve tickets antes que um humano os veja.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line148JsxAttrBodyAgentsAreOnlyAsPowerfu: Agentes são tão poderosos quanto as ferramentas que conseguem acessar. Nós conectamos todo o stack da empresa — codebases, CRMs, bancos de dados, plataformas de comunicação, sistemas de arquivos, APIs externas. Um agente que não consegue agir é apenas um modelo de linguagem. Um agente que consegue acessar tudo é um funcionário.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line153JsxTextGoalLoopTheAutonomousExecu: Goal loop: o loop de execução autônoma. Um agente pega uma tarefa, trabalha nela até a conclusão, verifica o resultado por conta própria e só para quando consegue provar que a saída está correta. Sem condução passo a passo. Sem aprovação a cada etapa. O trabalho é feito, ou o agente escala com uma explicação clara do motivo.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line158JsxAttrBodyEverySessionEveryDecis: Cada sessão, cada decisão, cada correção é preservada. Os agentes lembram o que funcionou. Eles lembram suas preferências, seus padrões, seus casos extremos. Quanto mais tempo a fábrica roda, mais inteligente ela fica. Essa é a vantagem composta que humanos não conseguem replicar em escala.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line163JsxAttrBodyTheFinalLayerIsHumanGo: A camada final é a governança humana: revisar o que importa, corrigir o rumo, definir novos objetivos. Não tocar em cada tarefa. Não aprovar cada entrega. Governar o sistema. Esse é o 24% — e é o 24% de maior alavancagem que essas pessoas jamais vão investir.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line172JsxTextHighestConvictionFromHighe: Maior convicção a partir da maior exposição
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line178JsxTextWeRunOurOwnCompanyOnKortix: Rodamos nossa própria empresa na Kortix. Todos os dias. Nossa engenharia, nossas operações, nosso crescimento — agentes fazendo o trabalho, humanos governando o sistema. Consumimos nosso próprio output antes de entregá-lo a qualquer outra pessoa.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line186JsxTextThisIsNotAProductDemoItIs: Isto não é uma demonstração de produto. É como operamos. A credibilidade do playbook vem do fato de vivermos dentro dele. Cada falha que encontramos, corrigimos. Cada caso extremo que enfrentamos, documentamos. A fábrica melhora a si mesma.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line203JsxTextKortixIsInfrastructureTheP: Kortix é infraestrutura. A plataforma não é o ponto.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line209JsxTextThePointIsTheMigrationFrom: O ponto é a migração — de operadas por humanos para operadas por IA. De empresas que exigem atenção humana constante para continuar funcionando, para empresas que operam sozinhas e só exigem julgamento humano para continuar melhorando.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line217JsxTextWeThinkEverySeriousCompany: Acreditamos que toda empresa séria fará essa migração nos próximos anos. As que se moverem cedo operarão com uma base de custos e uma velocidade de execução que os atrasados não conseguirão alcançar. Estamos construindo o caminho — e caminhando por ele primeiro.
appHomeFactoryFactoryClient.line228JsxTextWeAreASmallTeamWeCareThat: Somos uma equipe pequena. Importa para nós que o playbook seja real, que funcione e que toda empresa que ajudamos a operar dessa forma saia do outro lado mais rápida e mais enxuta do que quando começou.

appHomePage.heroDescription: Build, govern, and run AI agents that get real work done across your company every day.
appHomePage.workLoopTitle: From "do this" to done, with you in control.
appHomePage.workLoopDescription: Every agent gets the tools, sandbox, memory, and approval flow needed to finish a task. You see the work before it lands.
appHomePage.workLoopFinancePrompt: Pull every March invoice from Stripe, reconcile against the ledger, and flag mismatches.
appHomePage.workLoopEngineeringPrompt: Review this PR, launch the preview app, find regressions, and open fixes on a branch.
appHomePage.workLoopSalesPrompt: Find new fintech leads, enrich them, draft outreach, and queue only qualified accounts.
appHomePage.workLoopFinanceAsk: A teammate asks once in chat, Slack, or a scheduled run.
appHomePage.workLoopFinanceWork: A fresh sandbox opens, reads Stripe and the ledger, then writes the reconciliation report.
appHomePage.workLoopFinanceReview: Kortix shows every file, action, and decision before anything risky moves forward.
appHomePage.workLoopFinanceDone: Approved work lands in the repo, and the next run already knows the process.
appHomePage.workLoopEngineeringAsk: A pull request event triggers the agent automatically.
appHomePage.workLoopEngineeringWork: It checks the diff, runs the app, inspects the preview, and commits targeted fixes.
appHomePage.workLoopEngineeringReview: Developers review the branch, screenshots, logs, and reasoning trail.
appHomePage.workLoopEngineeringDone: The fix merges through git, not a black-box workflow.
appHomePage.workLoopSalesAsk: Sales defines the segment, exclusions, and approval rules once.
appHomePage.workLoopSalesWork: The agent researches accounts, updates CRM fields, and drafts channel-specific outreach.
appHomePage.workLoopSalesReview: Managers approve batches before sends or handoff.
appHomePage.workLoopSalesDone: Every run compounds the team playbook instead of starting from scratch.
appHomePage.splitCompaniesTitle: An AI workforce you actually own
appHomePage.splitCompaniesBody: A workspace your whole team can use from chat, Slack, or schedules. Specialist agents run in parallel, share company memory, and operate through the permissions, approval gates, and infrastructure you control.
appHomePage.splitCompaniesPointOne: Live across a department in weeks
appHomePage.splitCompaniesPointTwo: Your data, models, and deployment boundary
appHomePage.splitCompaniesPointThree: No migration before the first useful agent
appHomePage.splitBuildersBody: One repo, one kortix.toml, one OpenCode config. Every session runs in an isolated cloud sandbox on its own branch, then commits and opens changes you merge like normal software.
appHomePage.splitBuildersPointThree: Git handles review, rollback, and conflicts
appHomePage.companyAsCodeDescription: Every agent, skill, trigger, and policy is plain text in a git repo. Diff it, review it, roll it back. We believe every company will one day be a git repo. Kortix is how you start.
appHomePage.companyAsCodeBulletConfig: kortix.toml declares triggers, channels, connectors, approvals, and sandboxes from the first commit.
appHomePage.companyAsCodeBulletAgents: Agents and skills are markdown under .opencode/ - edit and ship them like any codebase.
appHomePage.companyAsCodeBulletGit: Every change is a git commit: open a PR, review the diff, roll back instantly.
appHomePage.companyAsCodeBulletSelfHost: Self-host on your own infra, or run on Kortix cloud - bring your own models either way.
appHomePage.integrationsDescription: Connect Gmail, Stripe, Slack, GitHub, and 3,000+ more once. Every agent shares scoped, governed access through a single broker. One credential flow, not fifty. Add any MCP, OpenAPI, or GraphQL source.
appHomePage.enterpriseTitle: Secure enough to run the whole company.
appHomePage.enterpriseDescription: Fine-grained control over who and which agent can do what, with isolation, approvals, deployment options, and audit trails built into the operating layer.
appHomePage.enterpriseScopedDescription: Members, groups, and roles. Every permission scoped to people and agents alike.
appHomePage.enterpriseApprovalsDescription: Guardrails on every action - require a human to approve anything risky or over-limit.
appHomePage.enterpriseIsolationDescription: Each session runs in its own secure, ephemeral sandbox. No shared state, no blast radius.
appHomePage.enterpriseDeployDescription: Cloud, your VPC, or air-gapped. Encrypted secrets, full audit trail, data never leaves your perimeter.
appHomePage.splitDescription: Start with the same operating layer, then choose the entry point that matches how your team wants to adopt AI work.

appHelpCreditsPage.line34JsxTextLearnHowCreditsWorkAndHowTh: Entenda como os créditos funcionam e como são consumidos
appHelpCreditsPage.line42JsxTextCreditsAreKortixSStandardUn: Créditos são a unidade padrão de medida da Kortix para uso da plataforma. Pense neles como tokens que alimentam seus agentes de IA: quanto mais complexa ou longa for a tarefa, mais créditos ela exige.
appHelpCreditsPage.line49JsxTextKortixUsesTwoTypesOfCredits: A Kortix usa dois tipos de créditos para dar flexibilidade na gestão do seu uso:
appHelpCreditsPage.line69JsxTextTheseCreditsAreIncludedWith: Esses créditos estão incluídos na sua assinatura paga e são renovados automaticamente todos os meses na data da sua assinatura. Eles expiram ao final de cada ciclo de cobrança e são sempre consumidos antes de qualquer crédito sem vencimento.
appHelpCreditsPage.line92JsxTextTheseCreditsNeverExpireAndC: Esses créditos nunca expiram e são acumulados mês a mês. Eles incluem compras de recarga, reembolsos e créditos promocionais. Créditos sem vencimento só são usados depois que seus créditos com vencimento se esgotam.
appHelpCreditsPage.line103JsxTextWhenYouUseKortixExpiringCr: Ao usar a Kortix, os créditos com vencimento são consumidos primeiro. Só depois que seus créditos com vencimento acabarem é que os créditos sem vencimento serão usados.
appHelpCreditsPage.line112JsxTextCreditsAreConsumedBasedOnT: Os créditos são consumidos com base nos recursos que seus agentes de IA usam:
appHelpCreditsPage.line125JsxTextThePrimaryDriverOfCreditCo: O principal fator de consumo de créditos
appHelpCreditsPage.line132JsxTextDifferentAiModelsHaveDiffe: Diferentes modelos de IA têm custos diferentes com base em suas capacidades e no uso de tokens. Os créditos são consumidos por tokens de entrada (seus prompts e contexto), tokens de saída (respostas do agente) e variam conforme a categoria do modelo (GPT-4, Claude etc.).
appHelpCreditsPage.line148JsxTextPlatformRatesVaryByService: As tarifas da plataforma variam conforme o tipo de serviço
appHelpCreditsPage.line156JsxTextWeApplyAMarkupOnTopOfProvi: Aplicamos uma margem sobre os custos dos provedores para cobrir a infraestrutura da plataforma, segurança e desenvolvimento contínuo. As tarifas variam conforme o tipo de serviço:
appHelpCreditsPage.line162JsxTextText20MarkupOnAllLlmApiCos: Margem de 20% sobre todos os custos de API de LLM (tokens de entrada/saída, cache de prompts).
appHelpCreditsPage.line166JsxTextText50MarkupOnWebSearchWeb: Margem de 50% sobre pesquisa na web, web scraping e outras chamadas de ferramentas de terceiros.
appHelpCreditsPage.line170JsxTextText100MarkupOnImageSearch: Margem de 100% sobre consultas de busca de imagens.
appHelpCreditsPage.line174JsxTextIfYouUseYourOwnApiKeyA: Se você usar sua própria chave de API, será aplicada uma taxa fixa de plataforma de 10%.
appHelpCreditsPage.line186JsxTextThereAreSeveralWaysToObtai: Há várias formas de obter créditos no Kortix:
appHelpCreditsPage.line199JsxTextIncludedWithYourPaidPlanAn: Incluídos no seu plano pago e renovados automaticamente todos os meses. Esses créditos expiram.
appHelpCreditsPage.line215JsxTextPurchaseAdditionalCreditsW: Compre créditos adicionais quando precisar. Eles não expiram e estão disponíveis para membros premium.
appHelpCreditsPage.line231JsxTextBonusCreditsFromSpecialEve: Créditos bônus de eventos especiais, promoções ou indicações. Eles não expiram.
appHelpCreditsPage.line247JsxTextCreditsReturnedDueToTechni: Créditos devolvidos devido a problemas técnicos ou tarefas com falha. Eles não expiram.
appHelpCreditsPage.line259JsxTextMonitorYourCreditConsumpti: Monitore seu consumo de créditos pelo painel de Configurações:
appHelpCreditsPage.line267JsxTextViewYourCurrentCreditBalan: Veja seu saldo atual de créditos e a divisão entre créditos que expiram e créditos que não expiram
appHelpCreditsPage.line275JsxTextTrackCreditConsumptionByTh: Acompanhe o consumo de créditos por thread e conversa para identificar os chats que mais consomem recursos
appHelpCreditsPage.line285JsxTextMakeYourCreditsGoFurtherWi: Faça seus créditos renderem mais com estas estratégias de otimização:
appHelpCreditsPage.line293JsxTextUseSmallerMoreEfficientMod: Use modelos menores e mais eficientes para tarefas simples. Reserve modelos avançados como GPT-4 para raciocínios complexos.
appHelpCreditsPage.line302JsxTextWellDefinedTasksReduceBack: Tarefas bem definidas reduzem as idas e vindas com o agente, economizando tokens e créditos.
appHelpCreditsPage.line311JsxTextRegularlyCheckTheUsageTabT: Verifique regularmente a aba Uso para identificar quais conversas consomem mais créditos e ajustar de acordo.
appHelpCreditsPage.line320JsxTextRepeatedConversationsInThe: Conversas repetidas no mesmo thread se beneficiam do cache de prompts, reduzindo significativamente os custos de tokens.
appHelpCreditsPage.line330JsxTextIfYouNoticeAnyDiscrepancie: Se você notar qualquer discrepância no uso dos seus créditos ou tiver dúvidas sobre cobrança:
appHelpCreditsPage.line355JsxTextWeReCommittedToFairAndTran: Estamos comprometidos com uma cobrança justa e transparente. Se você acredita que há um erro no uso dos seus créditos, entre em contato com nossa equipe de suporte e investigaremos prontamente.

appAccountsIdMembersUserIdPage.line210JsxTextThisUserIsNotAMemberOfThis: This user is not a member of this account.
appAccountsIdMembersUserIdPage.line253JsxTextWillBeAbleToDoAnythingInTh: will be able to do anything in this account, including managing billing and deleting the account. Only grant this to people you fully trust.
appAccountsIdMembersUserIdPage.line269JsxTextWillLoseTheBypassFromNowOn: will lose the bypass. From now on, every action they perform will go through the normal policy checks. They may lose access to parts of the account if no explicit policies grant it.
appAccountsIdMembersUserIdPage.line354JsxAttrDescriptionComputedByTheIA: Computed by the IAM engine — sum of explicit policies, group inheritance, super-admin bypass, and legacy role bridges.
appAccountsIdMembersUserIdPage.line435JsxAttrDescriptionAnyPolicyAttach: Any policy attached to one of these groups also applies to this member.
appAccountsIdMembersUserIdPage.line449JsxTextNotAMemberOfAnyGroups: Not a member of any groups
appAccountsIdMembersUserIdPage.line451JsxTextAddThemToAGroupToInheritIt: Add them to a group to inherit its policies.

componentsIamApprovalsCard.line117JsxTextWhenOnTheCuratedSetOfHighB: When on, the curated set of high-blast-radius IAM actions requires approval from a second super-admin before they execute. Requesters can't approve their own requests.

componentsIamAuditWebhooksCard.line103JsxTextShipEveryAuditEventToYourS: Ship every audit event to your SIEM or generic HTTP endpoint. Payloads are signed with HMAC-SHA256.
componentsIamAuditWebhooksCard.line400JsxTextOnlyDeliverEventsWhoseActi: Only deliver events whose action starts with this prefix. Leave blank to deliver everything.

componentsIamBreakGlassCard.line98JsxTextSelfActivateTemporarySuperA: Self-activate temporary super-admin for incident response. A reason is required and every activation, expiry, and revocation is audit-logged.
componentsIamBreakGlassCard.line134JsxTextNoBreakGlassGrantsOnFileAc: No break-glass grants on file. Activate one only during a real incident — every use shows up in audit reports.
componentsIamBreakGlassCard.line259JsxTextYouLlGetFullSuperAdminUnti: You'll get full super-admin until the grant expires or you revoke it. Every action you take while active is attributed normally — break-glass only adds permission, never anonymity.
componentsIamBreakGlassCard.line269JsxTextUseOnlyDuringARealIncident: Use only during a real incident. Activation is audit-logged with your name and reason.
componentsIamBreakGlassCard.line279JsxAttrPlaceholderEGP0BillingPipe: e.g. P0 — billing pipeline stuck, need to rotate secrets

componentsIamDriftCard.line115JsxAttrEmptyNoExpiredPoliciesToCl: No expired policies to clean up.
componentsIamDriftCard.line189JsxTextUseTheIAMAPIToEnumerateThe: . Use the IAM API to enumerate the rest.

componentsIamExternalGrantsCard.line72JsxTextGrantAConsultantOrPartnerFr: Grant a consultant or partner from a different Kortix account specific access here. They keep their own login; you attach policies normally on the member detail page.
componentsIamExternalGrantsCard.line91JsxTextNoExternalUsersAttachedUseT: No external users attached. Use this for consultants and partners who shouldn't consume a regular seat.
componentsIamExternalGrantsCard.line162JsxAttrDescriptionTheUserImmediat: The user immediately loses access to this account. Their own account is unaffected.
componentsIamExternalGrantsCard.line213JsxTextTheUserMustAlreadyHaveAKor: The user must already have a Kortix account. Look them up by email; you'll attach policies on their member page after.

componentsIamMfaRequiredCard.line119JsxTextWhenEnabledMembersMustComp: When enabled, members must complete a second-factor challenge before any IAM-gated action. Super-admins and Personal Access Tokens are exempt.
componentsIamMfaRequiredCard.line167JsxTextMembersWithoutAVerifiedSec: Members without a verified second factor will be blocked from every IAM-gated action until they enrol. CLI tokens (PATs) are unaffected.
componentsIamMfaRequiredCard.line185JsxTextNobodyWouldRetainAccessPro: Nobody would retain access. Promote a super-admin or have at least one member enrol MFA before enabling.
componentsIamMfaRequiredCard.line208JsxTextWillBeLockedOutUntilTheyEn: will be locked out until they enrol MFA:
componentsIamMfaRequiredCard.line234JsxTextWithoutMFATheyWonTBeLocked: without MFA — they won't be locked out, but consider asking them to enrol:
componentsIamMfaRequiredCard.line283JsxTextMembersWillBeAbleToSignInW: Members will be able to sign in with a password alone. Per-policy MFA conditions you may have set on individual policies still apply — only the account-wide gate is removed.

componentsIamPatPolicyCard.line98JsxTextApplyToPersonalAccessTokens: Apply to Personal Access Tokens (CLI / programmatic clients). Sandbox-injected tokens are exempt — their lifetime is bound to the sandbox itself.
componentsIamPatPolicyCard.line120JsxTextWhenOnTheMintEndpointRefus: When on, the mint endpoint refuses PATs without an
componentsIamPatPolicyCard.line137JsxTextRefusesPATsWhoseRequestedE: Refuses PATs whose requested expires_at is further out than this many days from now.
componentsIamPatPolicyCard.line151JsxTextTokensNotUsedInThisManyDay: Tokens not used in this many days are auto-revoked on the next sign-in attempt.

componentsIamPermissionBoundaryCard.line111JsxAttrDescriptionCapsTheMaxSetOf: Caps the max set of actions this member can ever use, no matter how many allow-policies they have. Super-admins are exempt.
componentsIamPermissionBoundaryCard.line120JsxTextNoBoundaryConfiguredAllExp: No boundary configured. All explicit allow-policies apply at full strength.
componentsIamPermissionBoundaryCard.line127JsxTextBoundaryIsAnEmptyListThisM: Boundary is an empty list — this member is effectively denied every action until you add prefixes or clear the boundary.
componentsIamPermissionBoundaryCard.line134JsxTextAllowedActionPrefixesUseAT: Allowed action prefixes. Use a trailing dot to cover a whole namespace (e.g.
componentsIamPermissionBoundaryCard.line234JsxAttrDescriptionRemovesTheBound: Removes the boundary entirely. All explicit allow-policies on this member will apply again at full strength.

componentsIamProjectGroupsCard.line76JsxTextBundleProjectsSoOnePolicyCo: Bundle projects so one policy covers many — e.g. “Mobile editors get Editor role on every project in the Mobile group”. Pick the group as the scope when creating a policy.
componentsIamProjectGroupsCard.line95JsxTextNoProjectGroupsYetCreateOne: No project groups yet. Create one, attach projects, then use it as a scope in any policy.
componentsIamProjectGroupsCard.line231JsxTextChooseAShortDescriptiveNam: Choose a short, descriptive name. You'll add projects to the group next.
componentsIamProjectGroupsCard.line346JsxTextProjectsInThisGroupAreCove: Projects in this group are covered by any policy scoped to it.
componentsIamProjectGroupsCard.line391JsxTextEveryProjectInThisAccountI: Every project in this account is already in the group.

componentsIamScimCard.line99JsxTextConnectOktaAzureADOrAnySCIM: Connect Okta, Azure AD, or any SCIM 2.0 provider to sync users and groups into this account.
componentsIamScimCard.line132JsxTextEachTokenAuthenticatesASin: Each token authenticates a single IdP integration. Rotate by minting a new one and revoking the old.
componentsIamScimCard.line153JsxTextNoSCIMTokensYetCreateOneTo: No SCIM tokens yet. Create one to connect your IdP.
componentsIamScimCard.line338JsxTextUsedOnlyToRecogniseThisTok: Used only to recognise this token later.

componentsIamServiceAccountsCard.line96JsxTextMachineIdentitiesForCICDAnd: Machine identities for CI/CD and integrations. Attach policies just like a member — pick the service account as the principal when creating a policy.
componentsIamServiceAccountsCard.line115JsxTextNoServiceAccountsYetCreate: No service accounts yet. Create one to get a bearer token your CI/automation can use without impersonating a human.
componentsIamServiceAccountsCard.line289JsxTextABearerTokenWillBeShownOnc: A bearer token will be shown once after creation. Attach policies to it from the member detail view (it appears under the Token principal type).
componentsIamServiceAccountsCard.line362JsxTextThisIsTheOnlyTimeWeLlShow: This is the only time we'll show
componentsIamServiceAccountsCard.line362JsxTextSSecretStoreItInYourSecret: 's secret. Store it in your secrets manager — we can't recover it if you lose it.

componentsIamSessionControlsCard.line148JsxTextCapHowLongABrowserSessionL: Cap how long a browser session lives, force re-sign-in after idle periods, and force-logout specific sessions. Personal Access Tokens are not affected.
componentsIamSessionControlsCard.line170JsxTextForcesAFreshSignInAfterThi: Forces a fresh sign-in after this many minutes, measured from when the access token was issued.
componentsIamSessionControlsCard.line179JsxAttrPlaceholderLeaveBlankForNo: leave blank for no idle gate
componentsIamSessionControlsCard.line184JsxTextKillsTheSessionAfterThisMa: Kills the session after this many minutes of no activity against this account.
componentsIamSessionControlsCard.line218JsxTextNoSessionsTrackedYetSessio: No sessions tracked yet. Sessions show up here the first time a member hits an account-scoped route while a policy is configured.

componentsIamSsoCard.line106JsxTextConnectYourIdPUsersSigning: Connect your IdP. Users signing in via SAML are auto-provisioned and their IAM groups are kept in sync from a JWT claim.
componentsIamSsoCard.line127JsxTextNoSAMLProviderConfiguredSe: No SAML provider configured. Set up the IdP in Supabase Studio (Authentication → SSO) first, then paste the resulting provider UUID and your domain here.
componentsIamSsoCard.line172JsxTextNoMappingsYetMapIdPGroupRo: No mappings yet. Map IdP group/role values (from the
componentsIamSsoCard.line173JsxTextClaimToIAMGroupsSoUsersLan: claim) to IAM groups so users land in the right group on sign-in.
componentsIamSsoCard.line254JsxAttrDescriptionExistingMembers: Existing members keep their access; new sign-ins via SAML stop being provisioned and group sync no longer runs.
componentsIamSsoCard.line343JsxTextCreateTheProviderInSupabas: Create the provider in Supabase Studio (Authentication → SSO) first. Paste the resulting provider UUID below.
componentsIamSsoCard.line379JsxTextUsedToRouteSignInFlowsForT: Used to route sign-in flows for this domain through your IdP.
componentsIamSsoCard.line409JsxTextWhenOffOnlyUsersAnAdminHas: When off, only users an admin has already invited can sign in via SAML. Group sync still runs for those members.
componentsIamSsoCard.line492JsxTextUsersWithThisClaimValueInT: Users with this claim value in their SAML token will be added to the chosen IAM group on sign-in.
componentsIamSsoCard.line508JsxTextExactCaseSensitiveMatchAga: Exact, case-sensitive match against an entry in the group claim your IdP sends.
componentsIamSsoCard.line526JsxTextNoGroupsInThisAccountYetCr: No groups in this account yet. Create one first.

componentsIamStrictModeCard.line118JsxTextWhenEnabledOnlySuperAdminB: When enabled, only super-admin bypass and explicit IAM policies grant access. Legacy owner/admin/member roles and project_members rows are ignored.
componentsIamStrictModeCard.line162JsxTextTheLegacyBridgesOwnerAdmin: The legacy bridges (owner/admin/member roles, project_members rows) stop granting access. Only super-admin bypass and explicit IAM policies will work after this.
componentsIamStrictModeCard.line180JsxTextNobodyWouldRetainAccessPro: Nobody would retain access. Promote a super-admin or create at least one explicit policy before enabling.
componentsIamStrictModeCard.line190JsxTextNoMembersWillLoseAccessEve: No members will lose access — every member is super-admin or has an explicit policy.
componentsIamStrictModeCard.line226JsxAttrTitleMirrorLegacyAccountRo: Mirror legacy account_role + project_members rows into IAM policies. Safe to re-run.
componentsIamStrictModeCard.line266JsxTextLegacyBridgesResumeGrantin: Legacy bridges resume granting access alongside your explicit policies. Owners/admins regain Administrator privileges, members regain account-level reads, and project_members rows act as fallbacks again. Existing policies are not affected.
